subject: Statistics
title: ARIMA Models
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on ARIMA Models.

hook.text: ARIMA sounds intimidating. It's just three ideas combined: memory, differencing, and error correction.

buildup.text: AR: past values predict the future. I: differencing makes data stationary. MA: past errors adjust the forecast.

discovery.text: ARIMA(p,d,q) — p is memory depth, d is differencing order, q is error adjustment depth. Three numbers, one model.

twist.text: Choosing p, d, and q is an art. Auto-ARIMA searches automatically, but understanding the ACF/PACF plots helps.

climax.text: Box and Jenkins popularized ARIMA in the 1970s. It dominated forecasting for decades and remains a benchmark.

punchline.text: Three letters, three numbers. ARIMA captures the patterns time leaves behind.

quiz.question: What do the three components of ARIMA(p,d,q) represent?
options: p = autoregressive memory depth, d = differencing order, q = moving average error depth
p = prediction, d = data size, q = quality
p = parameters, d = dimensions, q = quartiles
